raw_input: [auto-generated from playbook] Documentation software that feels human, not like fighting another clunky EMR with endless dropdowns and robotic templates
value_proposition: Documentation software that feels human, not like fighting another clunky EMR with endless dropdowns and robotic templates
pain_point: Current EMR systems force clinicians into rigid templates and unnatural language that takes longer than handwriting notes and strips away clinical voice
desired_action: Click 'Learn More' to see the interface in action
tone_direction: Warm technical friend—someone who understands both the clinical workflow and feels your pain with bureaucratic software. Evidence-based but never sterile. Like a UX designer who actually shadowed therapists explaining their solution over coffee.
visual_direction: Screen capture with warm color treatment: amber/cream borders, soft drop shadows, warm gradient overlay at edges. Show recording starting, real-time transcription, finished note appearing. Human cursor movements (not robotic smoothness). Handwritten-style annotations pointing to features. Clean but cozy.
key_phrases: Documentation that doesn't feel like documentation
Unlike your current system, JotPsych understands how clinicians actually talk
Record your session, get a complete note that sounds like you—not like a robot trying to pass a compliance audit
No templates. No clicking through endless dropdowns. Just real documentation.
emotional_register: From frustrated resignation with clunky EMR systems that feel like obstacles to care → to relief that software can finally match how clinical thinking actually works
proof_element: Shows actual product workflow in real-time: full note generated in under 60 seconds from session recording with zero template clicking
hook_strategy: Screen starts on typical EMR interface (generic, recognizable dropdown hell). Text overlay: 'This is what documentation shouldn't feel like.' Quick cut/transition to JotPsych interface with warm color treatment and smooth workflow. Immediate visual contrast.
target_persona_details: David, 45, PhD psychologist in group practice, tech-comfortable but burned by promises before. Daily routine: uses current EMR that his practice mandates, spends 20 minutes per client navigating dropdown menus to document a 50-minute session. Pain moment: 2pm between clients, clicking through diagnosis code trees for the 1000th time, thinking 'I have a doctorate and I'm basically doing data entry.' Wants better tools but skeptical of 'AI solutions' that are just chatbots.
source_pattern_id: Top Pattern #3: Screen Captures + Untested Combination #4 + Brief 2
